description_long: This bungalow features a low-pitched, dark-tiled hip roof with two visible chimneys and a wide, covered front porch supported by columns. The exterior walls are finished with a combination of light-colored plaster and a lower section of brick or stone cladding, giving it a sturdy and inviting appearance. Large rectangular windows are symmetrically arranged on the front facade, while the sides have fewer, more isolated windows. The front entrance is accessed by a short flight of steps leading to the porch, which is enclosed by a wooden or metal railing. The rear and side elevations are simpler, with minimal ornamentation and a focus on functional design. The overall structure is single-story, emphasizing horizontal lines and a compact, accessible layout typical of suburban residential neighborhoods.
description: A single-story bungalow with a dark hip roof, brick and plaster exterior, and a wide covered front porch with columns and railings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, wide porch with railings, and symmetrical window arrangement.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the hip roof, a single window, and the extension of the porch wrapping around the corner.
description_view_2: Direct front view with clear visibility of the porch steps, columns, and main entryway.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying a plain wall with minimal windows and the continuation of the hip roof structure.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical front-facing windows with isolated side windows
